what_it_does: This file is a living planning and audit document that consolidates work across three axes—channels, stores, and frontends—toward merging them into one Supabase-backed system. It records what is considered settled, flags genuinely new findings, lists open decisions for Tim, and drafts a provisional retain/replace ledger and merge proposal for multiple frontend surfaces.
observations: The file opens with a self-discrediting banner: it is provisional/raw input, written before a 'foundational transmission', reached Stage-0 conclusions that the 'Sequence law' forbids, and is superseded by a channel anchor.
It decompresses Tim's direction into a three-axis table: channels, stores, frontends.
It reports a new finding that the same human comment/annotation act is stored in three disconnected stores keyed by board://, ui://, and decision://, and proposes converging on cc_board.comment.
It states the channel-union crux as 'when a session ends, is the durable member the person or the session?' (handle vs uuid identity models).
It distinguishes two Supabase design threads: the internal union store (Postgres+pgvector, registries in Git) and a separate client-exposure/auth posture (RLS, remote-MCP).

It includes a cross-repo frontend census with a retain/replace ledger for surfaces such as canvas/app, surface/app, doc_review_server, DNA design layer, ConceptV, Visual Designer, blueprint, wildcard modes, graph-of-minds, and voice UI.

It synthesizes a provisional merge proposal: Instrument/surface/app as backbone, wildcard element-bound capture as the directable layer, modes as projections, tldraw composition as one mode, one reconciled design system.
It surfaces four open decisions for Tim, including channel identity, Supabase scope, and design-system token reconciliation (DS1).
It repeatedly notes verification limits: read-and-traced not run, Explore-agent census excerpt-level, counts unverified, some token reads likely stale.
standouts: The explicit self-discreditation and 'raw input only, not conclusions' framing at the top.
The three disconnected comment/annotation stores finding and the convergence prescription onto the existing cc_board.comment spine.
The distinction between the union-store Supabase thread and the client-exposure/auth Supabase thread.
The provisional retain/replace frontend ledger and merge proposal.
The DS1 design-system token reconciliation note, correcting an earlier stale read.
conventions: Markdown structure with banners, headings, tables, blockquotes, and a RELATES section.
Address-scheme vocabulary: board://, ui://, decision://, channel://, session://, clone://, mind://.
Status annotations: OPEN, discredited/provisional, living doc, maintained-by-integration.
Honesty caveats about verification method and supersession.
References to other files and channel anchors using path and channel:// / board:// URIs.
Use of tables to map axes, stores, frontends, retain/replace qualities, and open decisions.
concerns: The document is explicitly marked provisional/discredited and may contain stale or unverified claims, so it should not be treated as conclusions.
The frontend census is excerpt-level and unverified; specific counts and characterizations are flagged as unreliable.
It mixes raw findings with a synthesized merge proposal that is itself provisional and pending Tim decisions.
It leaves multiple load-bearing questions unresolved (channel identity, Supabase scope, canonical design-system look).
It references laws/transmissions (Sequence law, Stage-0, Tim's foundational transmission) that are not defined inside this file.
notes: A self-flagged provisional consolidation brief that maps channels, stores, and frontends, highlights a comment-store divergence, and drafts a frontend merge proposal plus open Tim decisions.
questions: What is the 'foundational transmission' and the 'Sequence law' mentioned in the banner?
What are the exact current values of the counterpart DNA tokens, given the in-repo tokens are marked final gold-warm?
Is the intended 'one Supabase backend' the union store, the client-exposure/auth layer, or both fused?
How will the channel member identity crux (person vs session) be resolved?
What is the 'total-coverage ledger-graph' that will supersede this document?
What is the precise scope and content of the 'client-exposure / auth posture' migrations?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to consolidate three axes into one system and record findings for deeper passes. It does that, but it is heavily framed as provisional/raw input with many unverified items and open decisions, and its synthesized merge proposal is explicitly not final.
apparent_themes: Address-driven architecture and registry-in-Git vs store-in-SQL separation.
Honesty about verification limits and supersession.
Tim-gated decisions for load-bearing identity/scope/design questions.
Convergence onto existing spine primitives instead of inventing new machinery.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to avoid re-deriving already-settled work, keep raw findings and provisional conclusions maintained and visible, tee up genuine decisions for Tim, and sketch a build shape that renders unrendered surfaces and wires them to a unified backend while preserving each frontend's unique qualities.
novelty: The three-axis consolidation framing, the explicit retain/replace ledger for frontend merge, the self-discredited 'raw input only' stance, and the distinction between two Supabase design threads.
contribution: A structured consolidation brief that surfaces the comment/annotation store divergence, the channel identity crux, the dual Supabase thread tension, a cross-repo frontend census with retain/replace qualities, and a provisional merge proposal plus open decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A provisional living markdown document consolidating channels, stores, and frontend surfaces into one Supabase-backed system, recording findings, a retain/replace frontend ledger, and open Tim decisions.
intention_for_embedding: To capture Tim's merge-and-consolidate direction across three axes and provide a maintained staging point for deeper passes and Tim decisions.
